channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Deepfake porn is a labor issue
item.description: Last month, First Lady Melania Trump used her first public remarks of President Trump’s second term to voice her support for the Take It Down Act, a bipartisan bill aimed at curbing deepfake “revenge porn.” She was joined by Elliston Berry, a Texas teenager who was a victim of deepfake porn.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Wohl, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE is ditching this analog file storage system. That could spell bad news for data integrity
item.description: As Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) continues to reshape the U.S. government’s digital infrastructure—scrapping websites, eliminating jobs, and dissolving entire departments—archivists have been racing to preserve a vanishing record of public history.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Sparq wants drivers to be their own AI-powered mechanics
item.description: Cars are about to get a lot more expensive. This startup wants to make your services cheaper. Sparq Diagnostics, cofounded by Codrin Cobzaru and Daniel Nieh, has developed a plug-in scanner designed for everyday drivers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Big San Jose industrial complex where hundreds could work pushes ahead
item.description: A big industrial complex in north San Jose that could accommodate hundreds of jobs has been approved.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: UALink Consortium Publishes Open Standard Interconnect for AI Clusters
item.description: UALink consortium publishes open standard interconnect for AI clusters, providing alternative to Nvidia's NVLink
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: You might get a free Meta Quest 3 to use on your next flight, but I'm not keen on the advertising it'll serve you
item.description: The next time you fly you could be handed a Meta Quest 3 to keep you entertained. Just look out for the ads.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Google to add on-demand genAI data analyst to Workspace
item.description: Google’s new on-demand generative AI (genAI) analyst for spreadsheets stood out among a slew of new AI features for its Workplace productivity suite announced at this week’s Cloud Next event in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Unveils Open Protocol to Connect Agents Across Ecosystems
item.description: Google has introduced a new open protocol called Agent2Agent (A2A) that enables enterprises to adopt agents more readily by bypassing the challenge of agents built on different vendor ecosystems not being able to communicate with each other.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google Unveils Open Source Framework for Building AI Agents
item.description: Google adds open source Agent Development Kit (ADK) to Vertex AI, along with other updates to help deploy and maintain AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google Cloud introduces cloud app design center
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Application Design Center, a service that helps platform administrators and developers design, deploy, and manage applications on the Google Cloud Platform. Application Design Center is in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Samsung's Galaxy Watch sleep apnea detection could get a big future boost from AI
item.description: Samsung is partnering with Stanford University to try and improve its sleep apnea detection feature using AI.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Senators probe Google-Anthropic, Microsoft-OpenAI deals over antitrust concerns
item.description: Democratic Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden have launched a formal inquiry into partnerships between tech giants Google and Microsoft, and AI startups, demanding detailed information about arrangements they fear may be circumventing antitrust scrutiny while consolidating power in the rapidly
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A wild new way to think about Android apps
item.description: Fair warning: This column is a fair bit different from the type of thinking we typically take on in these musty virtual quarters of ours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Four paradoxes of software development
item.description: Civil engineers can rightfully claim that no two bridges are exactly alike. However, bridges share many known characteristics, and the materials they are built with have known characteristics. Building bridges involves many known knowns and not as many unknown unknowns as one might think.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano spews fountains of lava in latest eruption episode
item.description: Scientists are bracing for high lava fountains in the latest episode of an ongoing eruption of Hawaii’s Kilauea volcano.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One in six workers would quit if forced to return to office
item.description: The American workforce is undergoing a shift, with major companies like Amazon, Apple, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan and Tesla enforcing return-to-office (RTO) mandates, leading to fewer remote work opportunities for employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: CrushFTP vulnerability exploited in the wild, added to CISA KEV database
item.description: A new patch for a managed file transfer software CrushFTP was released, and users are urged to apply it ASAP.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: HPE Aruba grows network mgmt. deployment options
item.description: HPE Aruba this week made its cloud-based network management and orchestration platform available via new deployment options, offering customers virtual private cloud (VPC) and on-premises alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s historic selloff has bulls balking from tariff risks
item.description: Apple shares dropped 19% in the wake of last week’s tariffs announcement, marking the worst three-day stretch for Apple since 2001.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 7 risk management rules every CIO should follow
item.description: Risk is inescapable. Look around and you’ll see technological, economic, and competitive obstacles that CIOs must not only handle, but defeat.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Visual Studio Code stabilizes agent mode
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.99, the latest release of Microsoft’s popular code editor, is now available. Highlights of the update center on GitHub Copilot agent mode, Next Edit Suggestions, and Copilot chat. Developers can access Visual Studio Code 1.99, also known as the March 2025 release, through code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SpaceX tourists return to Earth after a private trip that orbited the poles
item.description: Four space tourists who orbited the north and south poles returned to Earth on Friday, splashing down in the Pacific to end their privately funded polar tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk, Navarro tangle on tariffs
item.description: Tech billionaire and close Trump ally Elon Musk tangled with the president's trade adviser Peter Navarro on tariffs over the weekend, as the administration’s new import taxes have roiled the stock market.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Dangers of oversharing with AI tools
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says ChatGPT learns from chats, but you should avoid sharing sensitive information to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to free up space on your Android
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers some of his easy expert tips to free up storage space on your Android if your device is running short on storage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ivanti warns customers of new critical flaw exploited in the wild
item.description: Ivanti is warning customers that a critical vulnerability that impacts its VPN appliances and other products has already been exploited in the wild by a Chinese APT group.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ServiceNow to acquire Logik.ai to boost CRM portfolio
item.description: ServiceNow is acquiring Logik.ai and its configure, price, quote (CPQ) solution to expand its customer relationship management (CRM) product portfolio and accelerate its momentum in sales and order management. Logik.
item.dc:creator: Network World
